title: limnophila chinensis subsp. chevalieri
intro: Limnophila chinensis subsp. chevalieri, also known as Chinese marshweed, is a perennial aquatic plant species native to China. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Chinese marshweed include its potential to aid in the treatment of digestive issues, reduce fever, and support the immune system. Chinese marshweed is often prepared as an herbal tea or tincture, and it is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to promote overall well-being.
